meta.title: Overcoming Ammonia Synthesis Scaling Relations with Plasma-enabled Catalysis
meta.note: Numeric values read from Figure 5 (STY) are approximate plot reads; confidence lowered accordingly. Figure-based comparative claims (orders of magnitude) transcribed qualitatively.

claims.text: Plasma-induced vibrational excitations in N2 decrease dissociation barriers without influencing subsequent reaction steps.

location.quote:
We postulate that plasma-induced vibrational
excitations in N2 decrease dissociation barriers without influencing subsequent reaction steps.

claims.predicate: model assumption: alpha*Ev reduces N2 dissociation barrier (eq 6); rates for reactions (2)-(5) unchanged from thermal model
claims.scope: DFT microkinetic model of ammonia synthesis

claims.text: Plasma enhancement is predicted to be particularly great on metals that bind nitrogen too weakly to be active thermally.

location.quote:
We predict plasma enhancement to be particularly great on metals that bind nitrogen too
weakly to be active thermally.

claims.predicate: modeled plasma-on rate enhancement is largest for weak-N-binding metals (right leg of volcano)

claims.text: Ammonia synthesis rates observed in a DBD plasma reactor are consistent with predicted enhancements and predicted changes in the optimal metal catalyst.

location.quote:
Ammonia synthesis rates observed in a DBD plasma reactor are
consistent with predicted enhancements and with predicted changes in the optimal metal catalyst.

claims.predicate: measured STY trend across metals matches modeled TOF trend ordering
claims.scope: DBD plasma reactor, Fe/Ru/Co/Ni/Pt at 438 K, 1 atm

claims.text: Conventional Haber-Bosch ammonia synthesis requires elevated temperatures (~700 K) and pressures (~100 atm).

location.quote:
practical rates are only attainable at elevated temperatures (∼ 700 K)
and pressures (∼ 100 atm)

claims.predicate: stated_HB_temperature_K ≈ 700; stated_HB_pressure_atm ≈ 100

claims.text: The conventional Haber-Bosch process operates at 100-200 atm and 600-800 K.

location.quote: rival that of the conventional Haber-Bosch process (100-200 atm, 600-800 K)

claims.predicate: stated_HB_pressure_atm in [100,200]; stated_HB_temperature_K in [600,800]

claims.text: Promising yields of ammonia can be obtained at low temperature (400 to 500 K) and atmospheric pressure with a non-thermal plasma coupled to a catalyst.

location.quote:
promising yields of ammonia can be obtained at low temperature (400 to 500
K) and atmospheric pressure when a non-thermal plasma is operated in tandem with a

claims.predicate: literature reports NH3 production at 400-500 K and atmospheric pressure with plasma+catalyst

claims.text: N2 has a relatively large vibrational spacing of ~0.3 eV.

location.quote: relatively large vibrational spacing (∼ 0.3 eV)

claims.predicate: stated_N2_vibrational_spacing_eV ≈ 0.3

claims.text: Calculated thermal rates were computed at Haber-Bosch conditions (673 K, 100 atm) and target low T/P conditions (473 K, 1 atm); Ru and Fe are optimal mono-metallic catalysts at high T and P.

location.quote:
tative of of Haber-Bosch conditions (673 K, 100 atm) and target [9] low T and P conditions
(473 K, 1 atm) are compared in Figure 1.

claims.predicate: modeled volcano peak at HB conditions falls at Ru and Fe; conditions 673 K/100 atm and 473 K/1 atm

claims.text: At reduced temperature and pressure, rates on the same stepped surfaces are several orders of magnitude lower than the corresponding Haber-Bosch rates, and terrace rates are a further few orders of magnitude lower.

location.quote:
rates on the same stepped
surfaces are several orders of magnitude lower than the corresponding Haber-Bosch rates.
Rates on terrace sites are a further few orders of magnitude lower.

claims.predicate: log10(TOF) at 473 K/1 atm steps << log10(TOF) at 673 K/100 atm steps by several orders

claims.text: The bulk gas temperature measured by in situ thermocouple was 398.5 ± 5 K.

location.quote: temperature (Tgas), measured using an in situ thermocouple, was 398.5 ± 5 K.

claims.predicate: measured_Tgas_K == 398.5 ± 5

claims.text: An average value of Tvib = 3000 K was used in the model.

location.quote: An average value of Tvib = 3000 K was used in our model.

claims.predicate: model_Tvib_K == 3000

claims.text: The Treanor distribution predicts that only about 63.2% of N2 remains in the ground vibrational state.

location.quote: only about 63.2 % of N2 remains in the ground vibrational state.

claims.predicate: Treanor_ground_state_population_pct == 63.2 at Tvib=3000 K, Tgas=473 K
claims.scope: Treanor distribution, Tvib=3000 K, Tgas=473 K, Figure 3b

claims.text: The first vibrationally excited state has a population of 21.4%, and the tail states (v8-v10) have probabilities of around 0.2%.

location.quote:
The first vibrationally
excited state has a population of 21.4 %, while the states in the tail of the distribution (v8 -
v10) have probabilites of around 0.2 %.

claims.predicate: Treanor_v1_population_pct == 21.4; Treanor_tail_population_pct ≈ 0.2
claims.scope: Treanor distribution, Tvib=3000 K, Tgas=473 K

claims.text: The truncated distribution at the Treanor minimum includes the first 10 vibrationally excited states.

location.quote: imum (which includes the first 10 vibrationally excited states) to compute the normalized

claims.predicate: number_of_excited_states_included == 10

claims.text: For both step and terrace sites, optimal plasma-on reaction rates are several orders of magnitude higher than those for thermal catalysis.

location.quote:
the optimal plasma-on reaction rates
(i.e. the peaks of the volcano curves) are several orders of magnitude higher than those for
thermal catalysis.

claims.predicate: peak log10(plasma-on TOF) - peak log10(plasma-off TOF) >> several orders

claims.text: For step sites, the plasma-on volcano peak shifts toward weaker nitrogen binding, predicting Co and Ni near the peak.

location.quote:
the peak of the plasma-on volcano curve shows
a distinct shift towards weaker nitrogen binding energies, predicting Co and Ni to be near
the volcano peak.

claims.predicate: plasma-on volcano peak (step) located at Co/Ni binding energies

claims.text: Rates on the optimal plasma-on catalyst are comparable to those on the optimal thermal catalyst (Ru steps) at 673 K and 100 atm.

location.quote:
we find that rates on the optimal plasma-on catalyst are
comparable to those on the optimal thermal catalyst at 673 K and 100 atm, Ru steps.

claims.predicate: peak plasma-on TOF (473 K/1 atm) ≈ peak Haber-Bosch TOF (673 K/100 atm, Ru steps)

claims.text: On terrace sites, the plasma-on TOF on Ru terraces is predicted to be two orders of magnitude lower than on Co steps.

location.quote:
the TOF on these sites are predicted to be two orders of magnitude lower than those on Co
steps.

claims.predicate: log10(plasma-on TOF Ru terrace) ≈ log10(plasma-on TOF Co step) - 2

claims.text: The plasma-on TOF on Ru terrace sites is of the same order of magnitude as that on Ru steps, despite their plasma-off TOFs being five orders of magnitude apart.

location.quote:
the plasma-on TOF on Ru terrace sites is of the same order of magnitude of
that on Ru steps, despite the fact that their plasma-off TOFs are five orders of magnitude
apart.

claims.predicate: log10(plasma-on TOF Ru terrace) ≈ log10(plasma-on TOF Ru step); |log10(plasma-off Ru terrace) - log10(plasma-off Ru step)| ≈ 5

claims.text: The observed activity trend Co > Ni = Ru > Pt > Fe tracks remarkably well with model predictions.

location.quote:
the observed activity trend of Co > Ni = Ru > Pt
> Fe tracks remarkably well with model predictions.

claims.predicate: measured STY ordering == Co > Ni = Ru > Pt > Fe and matches modeled TOF ordering

claims.text: Measured site-time yields: Co ~0.063 s^-1, Ru ~0.027 s^-1, Ni ~0.027 s^-1, Pt ~0.016 s^-1, Fe ~0.011 s^-1.

location.quote:
Measured ammonia site-time yields in the di-
electric barrier discharge reactor on metal/Al2O3 catalysts as a function of the DFT-calculated
nitrogen adsorption energy.

claims.predicate: STY values read from Figure 5 for Co/Ru/Ni/Pt/Fe

claims.text: The plasma power was maintained at 10 W and the reactor temperature at 438 K for all kinetic measurements.

location.quote:
The DBD plasma was operated at 10 W and ∼ 20 kHz at atmospheric
pressure, and the reactor temperature maintained at 438 K.

claims.predicate: plasma_power_W == 10; reactor_temperature_K == 438; frequency ≈ 20 kHz

claims.text: Non-stoichiometric N2:H2 ratios in the range of 1:1 to 3:1 produced higher ammonia yields than the stoichiometric 1:3 ratio.

location.quote:
non-stoichiometric ratios in the range of 1:1 to
3:1 produced higher ammonia yields than the stoichiometric 1:3 ratio

claims.predicate: NH3 yield(N2:H2 in 1:1..3:1) > NH3 yield(N2:H2 = 1:3)

claims.text: The reactor configuration yields a discharge gap of 1.75 mm and a discharge volume of ~0.18 mL.

location.quote: configuration yields a discharge gap of 1.75 mm and a discharge volume of ∼ 0.18 mL.

claims.predicate: discharge_gap_mm == 1.75; discharge_volume_mL ≈ 0.18

claims.text: The Fridman alpha factor vanishes in the limit that dissociation is non-activated, as occurs at EN < -1 on steps.

location.quote:
α vanishes in the limit that dissociation
is non-activated, as occurs at EN < −1 on steps.

claims.predicate: alpha -> 0 when forward N2 dissociation barrier = 0 (eq 8); threshold at EN < -1 eV on steps

claims.text: Catalysts were pretreated in hydrogen at 773 K for 30 min prior to measurement.

location.quote: pretreated in hydrogen at 773 K for 30 min to remove any oxide layers

claims.predicate: pretreatment_temperature_K == 773; pretreatment_time_min == 30

claims.text: OES measurements used a 100 mg 5% Ni/Al2O3 catalyst under a 10 W DBD plasma.

location.quote:
reactor was packed with
100 mg of 5 % Ni/Al2O3 catalyst and operated under a 10 W DBD plasma.

claims.predicate: catalyst_mass_mg == 100; Ni_loading_pct == 5; plasma_power_W == 10

claims.text: Average metal particle sizes are based on at least 300 particles.

location.quote:
Average metal particle sizes are based on at least 300
particles.

claims.predicate: particle_count >= 300
